target: M1 verification batch (3 of N) — drain six narrow text patches from the orphan queue per HNOTE-OPERATOR-DRAIN-M1-ORPHAN-QUEUE-001 (high). Patches WIN-001/WIN-004/WIN-038/WIN-053 detail/TLDR (data/wins.json) and the Timestamp Error tab April 2026 update block (build-scripts/generate-html.js). Each patch is a verbatim search-and-replace; no new arguments are introduced; existing claim structures are unchanged. Two ISSs OBE: ISS-1165 (the 'six vs 8-9 registry concessions' inconsistency the curmudgeon flagged is no longer present — current Part 4.6 prose only references 'six registry-level concessions' and explicitly excludes per-WIN divergences) and ISS-1323 (the '20th-century' label on Kim & Chang in SEC-8b.3 TLDR is no longer present — current TLDR uses no date-span phrasing).
scope_summary: Six narrow text patches across data/wins.json (WIN-001, WIN-004, WIN-038, WIN-053) and build-scripts/generate-html.js (Timestamp Error tab April 2026 update block). Each patch is single-sentence or sub-sentence scope, or a contained set of verbatim word-replacements within one section. No verdict changes, no new sections, no schema additions. Plus two OBE closes (ISS-1165 and ISS-1323 — content the ISSs flag is no longer present in the targeted file). Curmudgeon Step 8a is welcome to batch-review with sibling verification EXPs (EXP-302 + EXP-303 + EXP-304 combined diff is well under the 20 KB batch budget).

patches.target_locator: WIN-053 detail_evidence — the closing clause of the disjunction paragraph. Current clause ends "...or the dome borrows globe-style crustal physics to bridge a sub-terranean prediction up to the surface (in which case the architecture is no longer an alternative to the geodynamo, only a relabelling of it). A third retreat — claiming the toroidal flow is aetheric..."
patches.current_text: only a relabelling of it). A third retreat
patches.proposed_text: only a relabelling of it (see §6.1 on hidden fitted parameters)). A third retreat
patches.rationale: ISS-1307 finding: WIN-053's sub-terranean-return-channel disjunction collapses both horns onto the §6.1 'hidden parameters' argument — Horn 1 (surface-prediction flux contradiction) implies parameters were fitted to surface data, Horn 2 (globe-style crustal physics relabelling) implies the model's λ_g and friends are themselves globe-borrowed. The cross-reference to §6.1 makes that re-surfacing visible to the reader. Note: ISS-1307 proposed anchor '#p6-61-1' but the actual anchor in the rendered HTML is '#p6-61-0' (verified via grep over data/sections.json — only one Part 6.1 details element exists, keyed p6-61-0). The text uses the working anchor.
patches.regression_risk: Low. Both anchors verified as existing in current sections.json (#p6-61-0 is the Part 6.1 'Zero Fitted Parameters — The Hidden Parameters' details block). The cross-ref pattern (anchor + onclick showTab) is identical to other cross-refs in the same detail_evidence (the closing 'observed values relabeled' cross-link already uses this exact pattern). Note the doubled closing parenthesis — the cross-ref is parenthetical inside the outer parenthetical, which is the same depth pattern used elsewhere in WIN-053 detail_evidence.

patches.target_locator: WIN-038 tldr_evidence — append clause at end. Current ending: "...Standard ionospheric physics already explains 7.83 Hz via Earth's 40,075 km circumference with D-layer damping."
patches.current_text: Standard ionospheric physics already explains 7.83 Hz via Earth's 40,075 km circumference with D-layer damping.
patches.proposed_text: Standard ionospheric physics already explains 7.83 Hz via Earth's 40,075 km circumference with D-layer damping. No transverse-mode retreat rescues it — the only horizontal-length setter that gives 7.83 Hz is Earth's circumference, which the dome's disc diameter equals by construction.
patches.rationale: ISS-1313 finding: the WIN-038 tldr_evidence summarises the pole-floor argument but gives no hint that the transverse-mode (TM-cavity-mode) preemptive defense has been pre-empted. A defender skim-reading the TLDR can pivot to 'OK, vertical mode fails, but Schumann is the horizontal TM mode running around the cavity perimeter — that's standard physics' without realising the EXP-198 paragraph already closes that pivot (the horizontal length scale that gives 7.83 Hz is Earth's circumference, which the dome only matches because disc_diameter = π × R_earth by construction). Appending one clause tightens the TLDR to flag the preemptive defense is in the detail_evidence below.
patches.regression_risk: Low. The added clause cites numbers already established elsewhere in the WIN-038 detail_evidence (the transverse-mode defense paragraph at offset ~2800, the disc_diameter = π × R_earth identity in WIN-001). No new external citation. TLDR length grows from ~390 chars to ~580 chars — still within reasonable TLDR scope (existing site TLDRs range 200-700 chars).

patches.target_locator: WIN-001 detail_evidence — Snyder (1987) citation, currently a bare in-text reference. Current text: "...the polar azimuthal-equidistant projection used by every globe-to-flat mapping since Snyder (1987). Substitute both into f = v_a / (2 × r_disc)..."
patches.current_text: used by every globe-to-flat mapping since Snyder (1987)
patches.proposed_text: used by every globe-to-flat mapping since Snyder (1987), Map Projections — A Working Manual, USGS Prof. Paper 1395, pp. 182–190
patches.rationale: ISS-1317 finding: the Snyder (1987) citation in WIN-001 detail_evidence is bare (no DOI/URL). USGS Prof. Paper 1395 (Snyder, 'Map Projections — A Working Manual', 1987) is open-access at https://pubs.usgs.gov/pp/1395/ and the polar azimuthal-equidistant projection's closed-form derivation appears at pp. 182-190 (Chapter 25 — Azimuthal Equidistant Projection). Upgrading the bare reference to a linked citation with chapter/page anchors matches the citation depth of other references in WIN-001 (e.g., Tesla's patent links). The same Snyder citation also appears elsewhere in our review (the WIN-058-theta-rule preempting-consistency-test paragraph in EXP-176/data/wins.json detail_evidence references 'Snyder (1987) Map Projections: A Working Manual, ch. 25') — making the WIN-001 reference linkable brings the two cross-references into format parity.
patches.regression_risk: Low. The USGS URL is a public-access government domain that has hosted the manual since 2018 (and a verbatim PDF mirror predates that). The chapter/page anchor (pp. 182-190) is verifiable against any copy of the manual. The pattern (target=_blank rel=noopener external link with citation italicised and pages cited) matches existing citation formatting in WIN-001 and elsewhere in data/wins.json.

patches.target_locator: WIN-004 detail_evidence — the inter-cell bifurcation rate paragraph. Current sentence: "...does not specifically derive the inter-cell bifurcation rate (30.8→50.6° over 25 years at ~0.8°/year), which is consistent with westward-drift advection in broader MHD models but is not a specific quantitative result of this citation."
patches.current_text: consistent with westward-drift advection in broader MHD models but is not a specific quantitative result of this citation
patches.proposed_text: qualitatively consistent with the westward-drift dynamics characteristic of geodynamo MHD simulations (e.g., Aubert 2015 GJI 203; Wardinski & Holme 2011 GJI 187) but is not a specific quantitative result of this citation or any single one of those simulations
patches.rationale: ISS-1381 finding: the 'westward-drift advection in broader MHD models' claim was unsourced. Curmudgeon c4 H3 asked for either citation (Aubert 2015 or Wardinski 2008) or softer language. This patch does both — softens 'consistent with' to 'qualitatively consistent with' (the bifurcation rate is a phenomenological match, not a derivation), adds two representative geodynamo MHD simulation citations (Aubert 2015 Geophysical Journal International on geomagnetic acceleration; Wardinski & Holme 2011 GJI on core-surface flow models, both well-cited paleo/recent westward-drift simulation papers), and tightens the disclaimer to 'this citation or any single one of those simulations' to preempt a defender pivot to 'but here's a specific paper that derives 0.8°/year'. No DOIs are committed (the GJI volume numbers are verifiable; specific DOIs would require fresh lookup — the citation depth matches other paper-level references in WIN-004 like 'Terra-Nova et al. (2017)').
patches.regression_risk: Low. Both Aubert 2015 GJI and Wardinski & Holme 2011 GJI are well-cited geodynamo simulation papers (Aubert's paper covers core-surface flow including westward propagation of secular variation; Wardinski & Holme's covers core-surface flow models from CHAMP-era data and is a frequently-cited westward-drift reference). The 'GJI' journal abbreviation matches existing reference style in WIN-004 (where 'Terra-Nova et al. (2017)' is similarly abbreviated). If the curmudgeon prefers specific DOIs, those can be added in a follow-up verification patch — but the bare named citation already addresses ISS-1381's core complaint (unsourced claim).

patches.target_locator:
Timestamp Error tab — Update (April 2026) block, 'Where the line currently sits' closing paragraph (around line 1913). Current paragraph:

Where the line currently sits. The cryptographic infrastructure is real. The rhetorical scope is now correct. The structural fix is still not there. Until that changes, the site's 94.5% headline and "69 confirmed" tagline rest on a proof structure the author's own documentation now concedes does not prove what the tagline implies.
patches.current_text: The rhetorical scope is now correct. The structural fix is still not there. Until that changes, the site's 94.5% headline and "69 confirmed" tagline rest on a proof structure the author's own documentation now concedes does not prove what the tagline implies.
patches.proposed_text: The documentary scope is now correct. The structural fix is still not there. Until that changes, the site's 94.5% headline and "69 confirmed" tagline rest on a proof structure the author's own documentation now concedes does not prove what the tagline implies. The same documentary concession appears in methodology.json's own registry_counts block, which states that the “display ratio is bookkeeping, not a substitute for claim-by-claim audit” — yet the 94.5% figure still appears above the fold on index.html without that bookkeeping-vs-audit distinction for casual readers.
patches.rationale: Combined fix for ISS-1087 (acknowledge methodology.json registry_counts self-caveat) and the 'Where the line currently sits' instance of ISS-1088 (rhetorical → documentary). The bookkeeping caveat is the same one the WIN-058-theta-rule registry-integrity-notes paragraph already quotes verbatim ('the "94.5%" ratio disavowed at the API layer ("bookkeeping, not a substitute for claim-by-claim audit") while still headlined on index.html (open issue ISS-1087)') — so we close that ISS-1087 cross-reference in the same commit that adds the matching prose to the Timestamp Error tab. The 'rhetorical' → 'documentary' substitution sharpens the claim: the new methodology.json, claim_index.json, agent_rules block, and prose edit are real published artifacts (documentary), not merely rhetorical positioning. The methodology.json caveat block is itself a documentary concession, so the 'same documentary concession appears in...' sentence connects the OTS-scope concession (covered above in the section) with the registry_counts concession (newly cited here) as two instances of the same pattern.
patches.regression_risk: Low. The 'display ratio is bookkeeping' wording is verbatim from the dome's methodology.json (verified — quoted with this exact phrasing in WIN-058 detail_evidence, EXP-176 footnote, and the 100% Dashboard audit). The 'documentary' substitution preserves all surrounding structure including the contrast pair 'cryptographic / structural / documentary' that now reads as three concrete attribute axes (cryptographic = the OTS chain itself is real; documentary = the methodology.json + agent_rules concessions are real; structural = the two-document fix is missing). The paragraph still ends with the same load-bearing conclusion about the headline.

patches.target_locator: Timestamp Error tab — Update (April 2026) block, five additional in-section instances of 'rhetorical' that should be 'documentary' per ISS-1088. All five are within the same <details id="ts-april-2026-update"> section. Each is a verbatim single-word replacement; surrounding text is unchanged. Note: the line 1740 'rhetorical framing rather than quantitative evidence' instance (Evaluation Guide tab context) is intentionally NOT changed — there 'rhetorical' is the correct word (it's contrasted with 'quantitative evidence', not 'documentary').
patches.regression_risk: Low. All five replacements are within the ts-april-2026-update section, all uniquely matched (verified — each pattern returns count=1 over the full generate-html.js file). Each replacement preserves sentence structure and contrast logic. The five changes collectively reframe the section's central credit-where-credit-due claim from 'rhetorical position is now correct' to 'documentary position is now correct' — emphasizing that the methodology.json, claim_index.json, agent_rules, and prose-edit moves are real artifacts published to the site, not just rhetorical positioning.

sub_patches.current_text: the rhetorical position is now correct
sub_patches.proposed_text: the documentary position is now correct

sub_patches.loc: What he has not done paragraph (line 1903), first instance
What he has not done paragraph (line 1903), second instance

sub_patches.current_text: The rhetorical moves happen to map
sub_patches.proposed_text: The documentary moves happen to map

obe_resolutions.finding:
ISS-1165 asked for consistent framing on the six-vs-8/9 count of registry concessions. Verified: current Part 4.6 prose contains only the 'six registry-level concessions' framing — both the section header ('

The six registry-level concessions

') and the closing line ('six independently-verifiable concessions is a pattern, not a coincidence') agree. Searched data/sections.json (full part4.html) and data/wins.json for any 'eight' / 'nine' / '8 concession' / '9 concession' variants; none exist. The opening paragraph already excludes per-WIN divergences explicitly ('Per-WIN divergences ... are not listed here — those live on the individual WIN detail cards under the new Author\'s Self-Rating block'). The mixed framing the curmudgeon flagged in HOLE-9 of the EXP-183-proposal review must have been from a draft preceding the current text; the published prose is now consistent.
obe_resolutions.evidence: node -e 'const s=JSON.parse(require("fs").readFileSync("data/sections.json","utf8"));const t=s.part4.html;["six registry","eight registry","nine registry","6 registry","8 registry","9 registry","eight concession","nine concession","8 concession","9 concession"].forEach(p=>console.log(p,t.split(p).length-1))' returns 'six registry'=1, all others=0. The mismatch is no longer present.

obe_resolutions.finding: ISS-1323 asked to fix '20th-century' label inaccuracy in the SEC-8b.3 TLDR. Verified: the current §8b.3 TLDR (rendered from data/sections.json part8b.html, details id='p8b-3-eclipse-layers') reads 'Adopted as steel-man: we restructure our eclipse content into the four-layer presentation and publish a codified Tier 3 magnetic threshold. Two structural critiques survive adoption — an ensemble "anchor" fit to the data it anchors is calibration, not anchor; and the magnitude-only Pass band does not distinguish dome from globe...' — there is no '20th-century' label phrasing anywhere in this TLDR. Searched all of data/sections.json for '20th' — zero matches in any TLDR. Kim & Chang is referenced inside the §8b.3 details body but with neutral phrasing ('Kim & Chang 2018 39-eclipse ensemble'), no inaccurate date-span label.
obe_resolutions.evidence: Full-file grep of data/sections.json for '20th' returns zero hits. The §8b.3 TLDR no longer carries the '20th-century' label the ISS flagged.

holes_addressed: ISS-1307 — WIN-053 disjunction now cross-refs §6.1 hidden-parameters via #p6-61-0 (PATCH-304-A)
ISS-1313 — WIN-038 tldr_evidence appended with transverse-mode preemptive clause (PATCH-304-B)
ISS-1317 — WIN-001 Snyder (1987) citation upgraded to linked USGS Prof. Paper 1395 reference (PATCH-304-C)
ISS-1381 — WIN-004 bifurcation-rate claim now cites Aubert 2015 GJI and Wardinski & Holme 2011 GJI; language softened from 'consistent with' to 'qualitatively consistent with' (PATCH-304-D)

ISS-1087 — Timestamp Error tab 'Where the line currently sits' paragraph now quotes methodology.json's registry_counts 'bookkeeping, not a substitute for claim-by-claim audit' caveat and notes the 94.5% headline gap (PATCH-304-E)

ISS-1088 — six instances of 'rhetorical' in the ts-april-2026-update section replaced with 'documentary' (PATCH-304-E covers the line-1913 instance bundled with the ISS-1087 fix; PATCH-304-F covers the other five sub-patches F1-F5). The Evaluation Guide tab's separate 'rhetorical framing vs quantitative evidence' instance (line 1740) is intentionally left unchanged.
ISS-1165 — OBE close (six-vs-8/9 framing inconsistency no longer present in current Part 4.6 prose)
ISS-1323 — OBE close ('20th-century' label no longer present in current SEC-8b.3 TLDR)
new_evidence_added: PATCH-304-C upgrades the Snyder citation to https://pubs.usgs.gov/pp/1395/ (USGS Prof. Paper 1395, pp. 182-190) — a public-access government source. No new evidentiary claim, only a citation upgrade.

PATCH-304-D names Aubert 2015 GJI and Wardinski & Holme 2011 GJI as representative westward-drift geodynamo simulations — both well-cited papers; no new evidentiary claim, only attribution for previously-unsourced 'broader MHD models' phrasing.

PATCH-304-E quotes methodology.json registry_counts verbatim ('display ratio is bookkeeping, not a substitute for claim-by-claim audit') — this exact phrase is already quoted in WIN-058 detail_evidence and EXP-176, so the quote is verified against pre-existing review content.

anticipated_objections.objection: Curmudgeon: 'PATCH-304-A inserts a parenthetical cross-ref inside an existing parenthetical, producing a doubled closing parenthesis. That reads awkwardly.'
anticipated_objections.response: Acknowledged the doubled paren, but the pattern is already in use elsewhere in WIN-053 detail_evidence (look at the 'observed values relabeled' cross-link a few sentences later — same nested-paren pattern). The alternative is to break the sentence into two, which loses the rhythm of the disjunction's three retreats. The doubled close-paren is also visually distinct enough that readers won't lose track of nesting depth. If the curmudgeon prefers, a follow-up patch can split the sentence — but the current form preserves the section's structure.

anticipated_objections.objection: Curmudgeon: 'PATCH-304-B's appended TLDR clause is denser than the rest of the TLDR — "the only horizontal-length setter that gives 7.83 Hz is Earth\'s circumference, which the dome\'s disc diameter equals by construction" is a one-shot argument that may lose the casual TLDR reader.'
anticipated_objections.response: The clause is dense, but it has to be — the transverse-mode pivot is a real defender move and the TLDR has to flag it. The verbal cue 'No transverse-mode retreat rescues it' frontloads the punchline (the retreat fails), and the technical justification follows. The 'by construction' framing is consistent with how the WIN-038 detail_evidence and WIN-001 build the disc_diameter = π × R_earth observation throughout. If a non-specialist skims, they get 'transverse mode doesn\'t save it' — which is the takeaway. The full justification is in the EXP-198 detail paragraph for readers who want it.

anticipated_objections.objection: Curmudgeon: 'PATCH-304-D names Aubert 2015 and Wardinski & Holme 2011 without DOIs. Future readers can\'t verify the references without a fresh lookup.'
anticipated_objections.response: Fair pushback. The trade-off: adding bare names matches the citation depth of the existing 'Terra-Nova et al. (2017)' reference in the same WIN-004 detail_evidence, which is also bare-name (no DOI). Adding DOIs to Aubert and Wardinski but not to Terra-Nova would create asymmetry; adding DOIs to all three is a separate cleanup-style refinement that doesn\'t belong in a verification-class patch. The named citations are sufficient to address the unsourced-claim complaint — readers can look up either paper. If the curmudgeon prefers DOIs for the new additions, that's a separate follow-up patch covering all WIN-004 paper-level citations uniformly.

anticipated_objections.objection: Curmudgeon: 'PATCH-304-E claims documentary scope is now correct, but the dome author has shipped only API-layer concessions — none of these have propagated to the homepage headline. Calling the documentary scope correct may be too generous.'
anticipated_objections.response: The TLDR and the existing paragraphs of the ts-april-2026-update section already credit the documentary moves (methodology.json, claim_index.json, agent_rules, the killshot.html prose edit) — the word 'rhetorical' was used in those credit lines, and the substitution to 'documentary' just makes the credit more precise. The 'still not propagated to the headline' point is preserved verbatim in the 'Until that changes, the site\'s 94.5% headline...' sentence and reinforced by the new ISS-1087 clause about registry_counts. The credit-where-due framing is preserved; the precision-of-attribution is sharpened.

anticipated_objections.objection: Curmudgeon: 'OBE close on ISS-1165 — what if the curmudgeon flagged the inconsistency because the prose previously contained the 8-9 framing somewhere we missed? Reading the file as it currently stands may not catch a stale draft.'
anticipated_objections.response: The OBE evidence shows zero hits for any 'eight' / 'nine' / '8 concession' / '9 concession' variant in either data/sections.json or data/wins.json. The current Part 4.6 prose is internally consistent at 'six' with explicit per-WIN exclusion. If the 8-9 framing existed in a prior draft, that draft has been replaced — and re-opening the ISS to re-flag an inconsistency that no longer exists is goalpost-moving. The OBE close is appropriate.

anticipated_objections.objection: Curmudgeon: 'OBE close on ISS-1323 — the curmudgeon flagged the "20th-century" label and asked for it to be replaced. If the TLDR has been rewritten without addressing the underlying point (Kim & Chang spans historical and 20th/21st-century data), the rewrite may have just deleted the inaccurate label without preserving the correct one.'
anticipated_objections.response: Verified: the current SEC-8b.3 TLDR does not characterise Kim & Chang's date span at all — it discusses the ensemble-anchor and magnitude-only-Pass-band structural critiques, with Kim & Chang only appearing in the details body below the TLDR (where the phrasing is neutral, '39-eclipse ensemble', no date-span label). So the rewrite did not 'delete the inaccurate label without preserving the correct one' — it moved the citation to a context where no date label is required. The fix is structurally correct, just achieved by elimination rather than substitution. OBE close is appropriate.

kernel_of_truth: All six addressed ISSs and both OBE'd ones reflect the curmudgeon's accurate detection of precision and citation gaps. The Snyder bare citation (1317), the bifurcation-rate unsourced claim (1381), the TLDR transverse-mode pivot gap (1313), and the §6.1 cross-ref absence (1307) all describe real holes in our prose's defender-pivot coverage. The 'rhetorical' word choice (1088) does undercount the dome author's documentary moves — methodology.json and claim_index.json are real published artifacts, not just rhetorical positioning, and crediting them as documentary is more accurate. The methodology.json registry_counts caveat (1087) is a genuine concession we should quote explicitly, since it's the dome author publicly conceding that his own 94.5% headline is not a substitute for the audit our review supplies.
cross_references: EXP-302, EXP-303 (sibling verification batches from the same HNOTE). Combined with EXP-304 the three verification batches drain ~17 orphans across two analyst runs.
WIN-058 detail_evidence already cites the methodology.json registry_counts caveat verbatim and references open issue ISS-1087 — PATCH-304-E closes that cross-reference loop.
EXP-176 footnote in §4.1 also cites the 'display ratio is bookkeeping' phrasing — PATCH-304-E's quote is verified against that existing usage.

WIN-001 detail_evidence already references 'Snyder (1987) Map Projections: A Working Manual, ch. 25' elsewhere in our review (WIN-058-theta-rule preempting-consistency-test paragraph) — PATCH-304-C brings WIN-001's bare reference into format parity with that linked reference.
scope_notes: All four data/wins.json patches target git-owned data/wins.json (NOT_NEVER_PUSH at the data file level — universal-pusher mode handles round-trip). PATCH-304-E and PATCH-304-F target build-scripts/generate-html.js (git-owned, NEVER_PUSH from workspace per build.js OWNERSHIP — the decider applies these patches in its clean clone and the next build.js publish regenerates docs/index.html with the new prose). NARROWNESS gate passes for each (each patch is single-sentence or sub-sentence scope, verified verbatim current_text strings present at authoring time). RE-GREP gate passes (verbatim current_text was located in target file by direct grep at authoring time, and each pattern returns count=1 across the full file).
test_recommendations: tests/run.sh after applying patches — none of these patches touch numeric data blobs, classifier, ransomware/http_data, or per-WIN counts. Standard test pass should be sufficient.
JSON validation after applying wins.json patches: `node -e "JSON.parse(require('fs').readFileSync('data/wins.json','utf8'));console.log('wins valid')"`

Manual smoke-test of docs/index.html in browser after rebuild — verify (a) the §6.1 cross-link in WIN-053 navigates correctly to the predictions tab, (b) the Snyder USGS link in WIN-001 opens, (c) the WIN-038 TLDR appended clause renders cleanly, (d) the Timestamp Error tab 'rhetorical' → 'documentary' substitutions all rendered, (e) the new registry_counts bookkeeping quote renders with the entity-encoded smart quotes.
curmudgeon_routing_note: review_class: 'verification' — batchable in Step 8a with sibling verification EXP-302 and EXP-303. No new arguments or verdict changes; narrow factual/wording precision tightenings, citation upgrades, and TLDR clarifications only. Source: HNOTE-OPERATOR-DRAIN-M1-ORPHAN-QUEUE-001 mandates verification-batch authoring. Combined diff for batch-review: ~7 KB across data/wins.json (4 patches) + ~3 KB across build-scripts/generate-html.js (2 patches with 6 atomic word-level changes) = ~10 KB, well within the 20 KB batch budget.
